reviews.review_msg: This is not a professor I would take again.
Enda carried such a positive energy in the lectures and made them very enjoyable! Assignments weren't too time consuming however the TA's were very strict and tough graders.
Enda is really nice. The TA ruined this class for me. The discussion posts are worth 25% of our final grade, and the TA is so harsh with marking.

Enda is very kind! As most people have said, this course started off being very disorganized. But Enda himself is a great lecturer, and he is very easy to reach out to when struggling with anything. The TA's on the other hand have ruined my enjoyment of this class, but that has nothing to do with Enda :)

Super kind, and has a really great energy in his lecture. The beginning of this course was difficult, and a bit disorganized (gave us extra time to complete stuff tho!) but his lectures are encouraging. quizzes are out of 4, and are just general questions related to the 45m-1hr lecture he posts :) Takes a day max to complete weekly stuff!
Enda himself is very kind, but the TAs are quite strict.

The professor is friendly and replies to emails quickly. The TA is careless. You are required to complete approximately 300-500 word reflection essays and short quizzes each week. There is a lot of reading material, the knowledge are useful but complex, so be prepared to read and write. The essays for the midterm is hard. Schedule is disorganized.

Course is so disorganized and the TAs are terrible. Lectures were posted late and assignments are vague. Grading is tough, and although we were supposed to get feedback on the first half of the reflections to improve, the comment I received was only three sentences long and not at all constructive. Completely lost interest in CMNS after this.

I'm writing this to warn others: do NOT take this course with Enda! He's completely disorganized and barely replies to emails. He forgot to upload lectures for 2 weeks, and the TAs don't respond either. We're 6 weeks in, and we have a quiz and essay every week. NOTHING has been marked! Nobody knows how they're doing. Take this with someone else!!
An amazing professor and takes the time to respond to questions.

Enda is great. He breaks things down so they're easily digestible, introduces current topics and connects them to the topics we study, and he's a good teacher! HOWEVER why in a 400 level course is a very large part of my grade dependent on a group project? I feel like it should be more about what you know rather than how well you work with others.

probably one of the best professors in the communication department at SFU. dedicated, friendly, helpful, everything you've probably ever wanted in a professor is right here. i took 130 with him as well and i highly recommend. participation is very very important!

His lectures are very helpful if you take proper notes of each and everything he says! He repeats himself and emphasizes important points. He is a very good prof. 240 is a hard course but I don't think he could make it any easier. He is funny too.

Best pedagogy ever. Interesting content and all-around informative. Lecture slides are a gem but his lectures are even better because he really knows how to teach for your understanding. I think he understands that international students struggle with English so he articulates clearly. I was marked by TA, not him.

Enda is a super nice guy, however, the lecture material can be pretty dry, but then again, it is an intro course. Wasn't a huge fan of the article analysis, and for the midterm, just know the slides, and final was kind of hard.

This review is based on taking his online class. I know that the TA is in charge of the course mostly, but it would be nice if Enda had left more clear and organized material to work with. Assignments are vague, "helpful" links lead to pages with one sentence. "Modules" just tell you what to read, no other guidance. Just me and the textbook here.

Enda is a great teacher who is, evidently, very passionate about this subject. Although the work load is relatively heavy (readings, exams, papers, etc.), it's mostly manageable. Make sure you can connect everything back to the PEC approach - critical to your success! Do the readings and attend lecture.
I actually enjoy this class a lot! Enda's sense of humor making the class even more interesting. If you're interested in politics and social issues, this is the perfect class for you!

Enda is approachable, and always more than willing to help. I have had him before, and he was great. However, the criteria in CMNS240 is vague and confusing. I did not have much interest in the topic, and the material itself is very boring. Enda tries making lectures interesting, but there is only so much he can do.  Also, he can be a tough grader.

Enda was quite honestly a sweetheart and I loved him as a person, however, this did not save his incredibly dry lectures. Attending them is important though because most of the important information is communicated verbally, and isn't on his slides. Readings are also very long and very boring but they are essential

Enda is a great professor. He can sound pretentious at times, but this was a first-year CMNS class and he usually teaches higher level courses so I'm sure it's not intentional! There's one writing assignment, one midterm, a term paper, and a final. Not too much reading, but don't fall behind. Enda is really funny and explains every concept well.

Personally I find Enda's lectures to be interesting and well organized. His lectures are easy to understand and straight forward. Readings are definitely dry but it's one of those things you just have to get done and over with. There is an article analysis, a midterm, a term paper and a final.
One of the best teachers. Really interesting and well-planned course.

Enda is a great prof. His lectures are very interesting and clear. Readings can be on a dryer side  but all the articles complement lectures very well.  CMNS 130 is an amazing course for everyone because it gives you a deeper knowledge about everything that happens around you. But be ready to work hard and don't fall  far behind the readings.

Enda is a sweetheart. Clear lecturer, super knowledgeable and passionate, brings in a lot of film clips to illustrate his points and brings in guest speakers every so often to bring in real life perspectives. He may lecture slow but he is so clear. Would love to take a class with him again.

He can be monotonous and talks very slow. Wish we could go back and listen to his lectures but they aren't recorded. What ruined it for me was the TA's -they marked this course like a 3rd year course and expectations were unclear. Class has article analysis assignment, midterm, paper, final. Exams were mostly reading based.
nice guy, clear lectures and slides. dont dig the midterm and that term paper tho
Enda is a cutie but his lectures are super dry and he talks super slow.  Good course though.

He doesn't record the lectures so if you skip one then you'll probably fall behind. He's also sort of monotone so it can get boring specially because this class is more theoretical. It gets sort of interesting if you understand the material but a lot of the readings can get complicated. Not awesome and not awful

Enda was super organized and always went over the last lecture and told us specifically what he expects us to know/ gain from the main topics. There is only a midterm, essay and final. There was a lot of emphasis on getting help if you needed it and Enda always allowed speakers to come in ! :) was interesting if you were into the material

I'm a first year, so it's important for me to have a good professor. Enda is extremely clear, and you can tell he is really passionate about this field. He also answers questions very well, and I don't feel as though I am being left behind at all, even though this is a lecture of 300+ students. I'm now even considering a minor in communications!
I found him to be very kind but some of his readings are hard to read though

Enda is a great teacher, but I just find the workload a bit heavy since I'm a first year & I am terrible at taking notes. He also gives you online pdf versions of the readings & study guide. There is also a paperback if you want it, but otherwise the pdfs are there.
Really enjoyed the class though it was repetitious of other classes in the CMNS field. Opened my eyes to some very interesting topics and loved his enthusiasm and passion for the material.
BEST PROF EVER! ENDA WAS SO HELPFUL AND CLEAR I don't see why anyone else would not like his lectures.Best prof I've seen so far.
Great prof, great class. Enda is clear and concise, honest and helpful.
Excellent speaker. His lectures are super interesting!
One thing I've noticed about Enda is that he really cares about his students. CMNS240 is by no means a super interesting course but I found his class to be very insightful.

Possibly the best lectures you will ever sit through. Unbelievably clear lectures that make excellent study material for the midterm. If you pay attention and take good notes, you will do well. Does a great job communicating information to students and structures the class well. Highly recommended prof.

Enda was VERY clear about concepts that you NEED to know for the exams/paper. The lectures are super structured and insightful. If you're interested in politics at all, you will definitely find this course interesting. I expected to dread this course, and it has become my favourite thus far!
Do the readings and pay attention, easy class.
Enda is just amazing! I took cmns 130 with him and he made the course really interesting . very helpful and clear lecture and super funny guy.

Enda truly cares about his students and the subject matter in this course. He's passionate about what he's teaching and it comes across in the way he conducts class. Extremely fair marker and always ready to help his students. Would definitely take another class with him - wish more CMNS profs genuinely cared about their students as Enda does!
his lectures are incredibly boring. He can be quite unclear. Slides are useless. Could be the bland course material though.

Enda is an extremely passionate prof but his lectures are hit and miss. There are some days that are really interesting, particularly the days he shows clips of documentaries (which is a lot) but when just going through the power point slides, class tends to drag on. Overall he really cares about his students and the class isn't difficult

Engaging prof, interesting, down-to-earth and clearly cared about the course material. He's funny as well. The course was interesting and I would take another course with him. The only issue I found was that the exams were a bit too difficult - they required analysis of the concepts that was far above what we were given the tools for.

Great prof - helpful, knowledgeable etc. I know some people got upset because they plagiarized and got caught. If you do your own work and make an effort you can get an A. Sometimes he talks too slow but at least he cares about what he's teaching! Loved the class.

he's a great speaker, explains material very clearly. willing to help and nice, funny at times.however you need to attend ALL lectures as his slides aren't that informative. overall he's a good prof, just that the material is extremely boring and dry.
Found his class, and lectures, to be v. interesting. He's stoked on the material, and it shows in his lectures. Also v. approachable.

Before I attended his class, I thought he was a women prof. But not, whatever. He classes were super boring. I remember the class of the semester he taught super fast and said "you wouldn't have to come this semester because I went over all the material in this class" LAME! He tries really hard to be funny, but he isn't.

Darn it, Enda seems like the nicest guy on the planet but he and this course just don't mix. He's a slow talker and sometimes this really exaggerates the dryness of the material. Slides are posted online but are not very informative - take notes. But he's a very passionate educator, and always willing to help.
Good prof, but horrendous course. It's unfortunate because he seems like he really cares about his students.
